message:
fix(io/buf_reader): clamp zero capacity to 1 to prevent empty-buffer stalls

BufReader::with_capacity(0, ...) previously created a zero-length internal
buffer, causing poll_fill_buf to loop indefinitely since read() into an
empty slice always returns Ok(0).  Clamping capacity to at least 1 prevents
this degenerate case while preserving the existing API contract.

- src/io/buf_reader.rs: `capacity.max(1)` in with_capacity constructor
- src/io/buf_reader.rs: add buf_reader_zero_capacity_is_clamped test
  verifying capacity == 1 after with_capacity(0, ...)
- src/io/buf_reader.rs: add buf_reader_zero_capacity_fill_buf_progresses
  test proving poll_fill_buf yields data after the clamp
- src/fs/buf_reader.rs: add test_buf_reader_lines_zero_capacity exercising
  the fs::BufReader wrapper through the full lines() pipeline with
  capacity 0, confirming end-to-end forward progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
